_source: Mock fashion trend data simulating a seasonal trend API response. In production this would be fetched from a real fashion platform.
season_note: Y2K revival and quiet luxury are the two dominant forces in thrift right now.

trending.notes: Baby tees, low-rise cuts, butterfly and star motifs. Huge on Depop right now.
Neutral tones, minimal branding, quality fabrics. Cashmere and silk doing numbers.
Flannel layering, combat boots, distressed denim. 90s band tees are the anchor piece.
Oversized fits, graphic tees, chunky sneakers. A perennial — not going anywhere.
Authentic 90s and 00s pieces with real wear. Buyers can spot reproductions.
Track jackets, windbreakers, color block. Champion and FILA moving fast.
Sports-inspired layering, track suits, zip-ups. Gorpcore adjacent.
Peaked last year. Florals and prairie still sell but slower than before.
Band tees and bootleg prints especially. The more faded, the better.
Authentic tour tees from the 90s-00s are fetching top dollar.
Wide-leg and baggy silhouettes. Skinny is fully out of the thrift conversation.
Clean lines, earth tones, versatile basics. Easy sell at any price point.
